title: [$250] Web app crashes intermittently with no console errors

labels.name: External

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description The web app is crashing intermittently with no errors visible in the JS console after the crash occurs. This makes it difficult to debug since the console state is lost on crash. Most likely this crash https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/APP-2BN ## Steps to reproduce Unknown — needs reproduction. The reporter experienced multiple crashes on the web app but was not able to capture console output at the time of the crash. ## Expected behavior The web app should not crash unexpectedly. ## Actual behavior The web app crashes intermittently. No JS console errors are visible after the crash (likely because the page reloads and console is cleared). ## Additional context - Consider leveraging Sentry for better crash reporting so we don't rely on users having the console open - **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778782686789009

title: [$250] Bulk expense edit hangs and crashes on large reports

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When a report has many expenses (40-50+), if you bulk edit them (e.g. toggle billable to false for all), the UI hangs noticeably and the browser tab can become unresponsive. ## Solution Two changes needed here: - The bulk edit flow makes an unnecessary report reload after each individual expense update, causing a flood of operations. This call should be removed. - The save button should show a loading spinner while the edits are processing so users know something's happening and don't keep clicking.

title: [$250] Connecting as delegate does not change the pusher subscription of the user channel to the new accountID

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/636456 # Problem We are noticing that after switching to a delegate account, you don't get any pusher messages for the account you delegated into. Sometimes, we also see `AuthenticatePusher` calls using the accountID of the original account, which fails with a 403 error. This also means that we see GetMissingOnyxMessages calls every time there's a new update and you do an API call, because the messages were not delivered to the app because it is subscribed to the wrong channel. # Solution It seems that we are not properly unsubscribing from the original account pusher channel and resubscribing in the new account channel or maybe we are and [this](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/1b89b63083d7ca1981db71f3ac0023441881967c/src/libs/Pusher/index.ts#L70-L73) prevents it from doing what it should. More investigation is needed

title: [$50] Flaky test: MiddlewareTest › HandleUnusedOptimisticID - fetch call count assertion fails intermittently

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description Two tests in \`tests/unit/MiddlewareTest.ts\` are flaky and fail intermittently in CI: - \`Middleware › HandleUnusedOptimisticID › Request with preexistingReportID\` (line 149) - \`Middleware › HandleUnusedOptimisticID › OpenReport to a chat with preexistingReportID and clean up optimistic participant data\` (line 343) Both fail on the same assertion: \`\`\` expect(global.fetch).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2) \`\`\` ## Evidence Observed in CI run [#33684](https://github.com/Expensify/App/actions/runs/25858979390/attempts/1) (attempt 1 failed, attempt 2 passed — confirming this is a flaky/timing issue). The logs also report a worker teardown leak: > A worker process has failed to exit gracefully and has been force exited. This is likely caused by tests leaking due to improper teardown. Try running with --detectOpenHandles to find leaks. Active timers can also cause this. ## Likely cause The tests use \`waitForNetworkPromises()\` / \`waitForBatchedUpdates()\` before asserting on \`global.fetch\` call count. A race condition between the \`SequentialQueue.unpause()\` call and the fetch mock resolution can cause the second fetch to fire after the assertion runs. The worker teardown leak suggests an open handle (timer or promise) that affects subsequent test isolation.

If you haven't already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/635728 ## Problem Icons in the Spend left-hand navigation bar are misaligned and badge counters have inconsistent widths across items, making the sidebar look unpolished. **Current state:** ## Agreed Design Solution The design team agreed on the following fix: - **12px right padding** for all LHN row items, including the accordion section title - **28px fixed-width bounding box** for both icon wrappers and badge/counter elements - All icons and badges **centered** within that 28px box **Design reference (video):** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f0969dc-d17b-46f2-a50d-244d1e3f7e0e ## Implementation **`src/styles/index.ts`** 1. `sectionMenuItem` (~line 6413): replace `paddingHorizontal: 16` with `paddingLeft: 16, paddingRight: 12` 2. `popoverMenuIcon` (~line 1841): change `width: variables.componentSizeNormal` (40) → `width: 28` 3. `todoBadge` (~line 5636): add `width: 28` to give row badges a consistent bounding box 4. `searchSectionBadge` (~line 5641): add `width: 28` to match **`src/pages/Search/SearchTypeMenuAccordion.tsx`** 5. Accordion header pressable (~line 95): replace `styles.p2` with `paddingLeft: 8, paddingRight: 12, paddingVertical: 8` ## Platforms - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari

title: [$250] Recipient field don't update to new WS name after upgrade and saving rate in manual track dist

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.74-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-28774/folder/18717689/49855697/1413061698 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+ck513261@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11/Chrome, Pixel 8a/Android 16 **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: 1. Use Expensifail account (or gmail account) 2. The user does not have workspace 3. User have Self-DM Steps: 1. Launch Hybrid app or navigate to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Login with new account 3. Click the + global create button, select "Track Distance", and go to the "Manual" tab 4. Enter a valid distance and click "Next" 5. Select the user’s email address(Personal) as the recipient 6. Click the "Rate" option row and click "Upgrade"  (A workspace will be created for the user at this stage) 7. Set a custom distance rate and click "Save" 8. Observe the To field do not update to new WS name. Instead, still Self DM displayed ## Expected Result: After saving custom distance rate, user get redirected to the "Confirm details" page and the recipient field should get updated to the new workspace name ## Actual Result: Recipient field do not get updated to the new workspace name after upgrading & saving custom distance rate. To field still display the Self DM, user has to select the created WS tapping To field for the manual distance expense to get create in new WS ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d903fbcc-1c64-4a90-8570-c31d21e5d1f0 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Make the Workspace/Domain member list multi-select, update `All members` to `Members`

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
We currently have a single-select list in the `Members` page of each Workspace and also for the `Members` page of each Domain. Changes required: - Update the list to multi-select - Rename `All members` to simply `Members` The driver is the upcoming release of more/custom admin roles, and the fact that it's already helpful to be able to select multiple role types within workspaces. We'll apply the same change to Domains, largely for consistency.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.74-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-28773/folder/13176725/41237082/1413059912?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11 / Chrome, iPhone 13 / iOS 26.5, Samsung Galaxy A52 / Android 14 **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Preconditions - Logged in to any user - Enable wallet on both account A and account B Steps: 1. Make sure user B is offline or logged out 2. As user A - create a chat with user B 3. As user A - Send a message to user B ## Expected Result: no email is sent to user B’s inbox after writing a message ## Actual Result: An email is sent to user B's inbox after user A send message to user B (in my case, the email arrived after 20 mins) ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24907599-c651-43c6-a294-65698f3e2d99 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
